message:
feat(wal): discard stale parallel-WAL batches after raw fallback; regression test for conflict-snapshot scan

Two coupled fixes on the cross-process conflict plumbing committed
earlier.

fsqlite-wal/src/parallel_wal.rs
- New `ParallelWalLaneStager::discard_batches_for_flush(&[TransactionFrameBatchContext])`
  method.
  When the group-commit pipeline falls back to the raw ordered WAL path
  (borrowed frame refs) after staging prepared payloads in the lane
  queue, those prepared payloads become stale — they reference frame
  bytes that the raw path will re-append, so leaving them queued
  permanently biases the front-of-queue batch id to an old value and
  forces every subsequent epoch back onto the raw fallback.
  The new method scans each lane's queue, removes any batch whose id is
  in the discard set, decrements the matching `lane_backlog_frames`
  entry by the removed batch's `staged_frame_count`, and trims empty
  lane entries. Locks are acquired in the same order as
  `record_batch` (batches first, then backlog) to avoid new lock-order
  inversions.
  Also tidies an existing brace scope in `record_batch` so the batches
  and backlog locks are held together — matches the new discard-side
  ordering.

- Three unit tests:
  - `test_lane_stager_discard_batches_for_flush_removes_stale_payloads`
    — mixed queue, discard the middle id, confirm the retained batches
    still flush with correct backlog accounting.
  - `test_lane_stager_discard_batches_for_flush_is_idempotent` — a
    second discard of an already-flushed raw-fallback batch returns 0
    removed and leaves the backlog at zero.
  - `test_lane_stager_discard_batches_for_flush_ignores_unknown_ids` —
    discarding an unknown id is a no-op and must not perturb queued
    batches.

fsqlite-core/src/wal_adapter.rs
- New `test_conflicting_pages_since_snapshot_detects_later_wal_writes`:
  pins the contract behind the `WalBackend::conflicting_pages_since_snapshot`
  implementation. Writes page 1, commits, pins a
  `TransactionConflictSnapshot` at that horizon, then appends another
  unrelated page and commits a page-2 rewrite. The test asserts that:
  - candidate `[2, 99]` returns `[2]` (page 2 was committed after the
    snapshot),
  - candidate `[99]` returns empty (page 99 was never committed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
